---
title: IBM Verify
---

Derived tables in the `ibm_verify` schema, populated from the IBM Verify
reporting API. Each table is a daily aggregate derived from the raw event stream
in `ibm_verify_raw.ibm_verify`. The field-level meaning of those raw events is
documented separately in the IBM Verify event data dictionary, organized by
event type (Authentication Event, MFA Authentication Event, SSO, and so on).

### Data retention

The dataset behind the IBM Verify reporting API retains only the most recent
366.5 days of data. This does **not** limit the tables here. The daily aggregate
rows are stored on our side and kept beyond a year, so any metric we calculate by
aggregating those stored daily rows (sign-ins, MFA attempts, and other additive
measures) is unaffected, no matter how far back the window reaches.

The limit bites only on summaries that the **API itself** computes, because the
API can only see the last 366.5 days when it does so. The cumulative unique-user
columns are the affected case: a count must be computed by the API rather than
summed from daily rows (summing daily unique users would count user-days, not
distinct people). The `mtd_` and `ytd_` windows fit inside the retention horizon,
but a "since the April 22, 2026 launch" total silently truncates once launch is
more than a year in the past (roughly late April 2027). The
`since_apr22_unique_users` fields that previously carried it have therefore been
removed from the pipeline.

{{< include ibm_verify/app_login_counts.qmd >}}

{{< include ibm_verify/auth_total_logins.qmd >}}

{{< include ibm_verify/mfa_activity.qmd >}}
